content.full_text:
September 20, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attention: Legal Division,
Peggy Bernardy

Dear Ms. Bernardy:

As I advised in our telephone conversation of Wednesday, September 19, 1990 I am a member of the committee to re-elect Mr. (Buss) Roberts as mayor of the City of Oroville. Specifically, I am involved in the fund-raising aspect of the campaign and I am concerned about the various restrictions involving reporting requirements and contribution limitations and wish to clarify these various restrictions prior to accepting or soliciting campaign contributions.

I would appreciate it if you would confirm my understanding of the following: First, that any campaign contribution of $100.00 or less does not require reporting the name and address of the contributor. Second, that any campaign contribution of less than $1,000.00 would not require Mr. Roberts to abstain from voting or otherwise participating in any council session in which a project involving said contributor is to be acted upon. In other words, it is my understanding that no conflict of interest requiring abstention would be created by accepting a contribution of less than $1,000.00.

I would greatly appreciate your written opinion on these questions at your earliest convenience. Thank you for your prompt anticipated cooperation.

Sincerely,

Rodney Mims
